creator: armin@frozen-zone.org
text:
The patch itself works fine for any platform i was able to test (6-9, i386 & amd64):
http://tb.frozen-zone.org/patches/patch-boost-1.43-1.45.tbz

boost-all: http://tb.frozen-zone.org/index.php?action=describe_port&id=21
boost-docs: http://tb.frozen-zone.org/index.php?action=describe_port&id=24
boost-jam: http://tb.frozen-zone.org/index.php?action=describe_port&id=23
boost-libs: http://tb.frozen-zone.org/index.php?action=describe_port&id=22
boost-pyste: http://tb.frozen-zone.org/index.php?action=describe_port&id=383
boost-python-libs: http://tb.frozen-zone.org/index.php?action=describe_port&id=382

However theses ports seem to have troubles building after the update:
audio/gx2osd ( http://tb.frozen-zone.org/errors/8.X-FreeBSD-PG-amd64/gx2osd-0.2_6.log )
databases/hypertable ( http://tb.frozen-zone.org/errors/8.X-FreeBSD-PG-amd64/hypertable-0.9.3.4.a.log )
games/alephone ( http://tb.frozen-zone.org/errors/8.X-FreeBSD-PG-amd64/alephone-20100424_1.log )
graphics/scantailor ( http://tb.frozen-zone.org/errors/8.X-FreeBSD-PG-amd64/scantailor-0.9.8_1.log )

(the other about 230 ports depending on boost build fine - except for the few which are broken due to gcc44)
